Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

LNet and libcfs need a separate value. The reason is that the native client version is always lower than the OpenSFS version. This ensures that if Opensfs modules are installed then get loaded instead of the native client.

Maloo Test Queue: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_queue?jobs=lustre-reviews&builds=111880&commit=Apply+Filter
Maloo Results: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/related?jobs=lustre-reviews&builds=111880#redirect

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> LNet and libcfs need a separate value. The reason is that the native client version is always lower than the OpenSFS version. This ensures that if Opensfs modules are installed then get loaded instead of the native client.

That's not the correct way to do that, IMO. We should use a targeted depmod config file. We used to use a global config file (i.e. https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/54025) which interfered with other modules. But if we make one that lists each Lustre module by name, we can get the desired effect without making the version strings wrong.

commit.subject: LU-18839 misc: update MODULE_VERSION strings
commit.message:
LU-18839 misc: update MODULE_VERSION strings

Several of MODULE_VERSION strings used in Lustre are very out-of-date. Use
LUSTRE_VERSION_STRING to replace all out-of-date macros. Keep LIBCFS_VERSION
and LNET_VERSION (and define them as LUSTRE_VERSION_STRING

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>
Change-Id: I341c096089d52e58cf089fa3a4a4ac89653dfc57
